title: Indulgent French Beef Casserole
description: A buttery, gooey slow‑cook beef casserole that feels like a French Provence classic while staying low‑glycemic. Creamy gravy and aromatic herbs make each bite luxuriously comforting.

ingredients.name: beef chuck, trimmed and cut into 1.5" cubes

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large Dutch oven over medium‑high heat.
Sear the beef cubes in batches until browned on all sides; transfer to a plate.
Add the remaining butter to the pot and sauté the onion, carrot, celery, garlic, thyme, and bay leaf until vegetables soften, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute to deglaze the pan.
Pour in the red wine, scraping up any browned bits, and let it reduce by half, about 3 minutes.
Return the beef to the pot, add the beef stock, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven; cook for 1 hour and 30 minutes until the beef is tender.
Remove the pot from the oven, stir in the heavy cream and Dijon mustard, and season with salt and pepper.
Simmer uncovered on the stove for 5 minutes to thicken the gravy slightly, stirring occasionally.
Serve the casserole hot, garnished with fresh thyme leaves.
